task_completion.description: Agent joined the specified Jitsi meeting room.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open to http://localhost:8080/QualityTestRoom and the Jitsi Meet interface is successfully loaded.
The connection statistics overlay or dialog is visible within the Jitsi interface, displaying network metrics such as packet loss, latency, jitter, or bitrate.

task_completion.description: Agent set the video quality to the highest available option.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The video quality or performance settings menu is open in Jitsi, and the slider or selection is set to the maximum/highest available quality (e.g., High Definition).

task_completion.description: Agent switched the meeting display to tile/grid view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jitsi meeting interface is displayed in tile/grid view mode, or the tile view toggle button is visibly activated.
The 'Speaker Stats' dialog box is open in the Jitsi interface, displaying participant names and their respective speaking times.

task_completion.description: Agent compiled the diagnostic report with all required information.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file saved at /home/ga/Desktop/meeting_quality_report.txt. The document contains the meeting URL, observed network performance metrics, the selected video quality, observations on the display mode, and speaker data.

integrity.description: The agent actually interacted with the Jitsi web interface to gather data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the Jitsi GUI in a web browser (opening menus, clicking buttons) rather than just writing a report from hallucinated data.

integrity.description: The report contains genuine observations rather than copied prompt text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text report should include actual numbers or specific states for the metrics (e.g., 'Latency: 15ms', 'Packet loss: 0%') rather than just repeating the prompt's generic text ('packet loss, latency, jitter, or bitrate').

integrity.description: The agent used a standard text editor or terminal to write the report manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report is seen being drafted in a text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) rather than being generated by an unauthorized external script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Jitsi Meet instance is hosted at http://localhost:8080. The target room name is 'QualityTestRoom'. The final diagnostic report must be saved exactly at /home/ga/Desktop/meeting_quality_report.txt.
